title: Deathlegion Problem Manifest Schema
description: Every problem in the Deathlegion Benchmark must have a manifest.json conforming to this schema.

id.description: Unique problem identifier. Lowercase with underscores.

title.description: Short human-readable title for the problem.

category.description: Which difficulty lever this problem exercises.

prompt_md.description: Relative path to the prompt markdown file shown to the candidate.

difficulty_rationale.description: Written justification of why this problem is hard, tied to one or more of the three difficulty levers.

near_miss_count.description: Number of independently-conceived near-miss solutions tested against the grader.

categories_tested.description: Failure-mode categories (from failure_type enum) covered by the near-miss set.

plausible_near_misses.description: Number of near-miss solutions that pass all visible samples and look correct on inspection.

passes_visible_only_solutions.description: Does the grader fail a solution that only hard-codes the visible sample outputs?

passes_degenerate_solutions.description: Does the grader fail trivial solutions (return constant, skip logic, etc.)?

source_repo.description: URL of the real GitHub repository (SWE-bench methodology).

source_issue.description: URL of the specific GitHub issue.

source_pr.description: URL of the merged PR that fixed the issue.

pre_fix_sha.description: Pinned commit SHA of the repository just before the fix was applied. Use a full 40-char SHA, not a branch name — repos drift.

fix_merge_sha.description: Pinned commit SHA of the merge commit that applied the fix. Full 40-char SHA required.

files_touched.description: Paths to files the fix touches.
